claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm enquiring about my son Alex. He's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information!

Since Alex is 18 years old, he falls into the category where Child Benefit can continue if he's in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or training?** For example, is he studying for A-levels, a BTEC, NVQ (Level 1-3), or similar qualifications at school or college?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and he's working full-time at a local shop now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know.

Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, it sounds like Alex may not be eligible for Child Benefit. This is because:

- For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit normally only continues if they remain in **approved education or training**
- Alex is 18 and has finished school
- He is now working full-time

Child Benefit stops when a child starts paid work for **24 hours or more per week** and is no longer in approved education or training.

Just to confirm before I make a final assessment: **Is Alex working 24 hours or more per week at the shop?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he's working full-time, so definitely more than 24 hours a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

I also need to check a couple more things:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just one more question to complete my assessment:

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live somewhere else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to make an assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to say that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

- **Alex is 18 years old** – For children aged 16 and over, Child Benefit can only continue if they stay in approved education or training
- **Alex is not in education or training** – He has finished school and is not currently studying
- **Alex is working full-time** – Child Benefit stops when a child works 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training

If Alex's circumstances were to change in the future – for example, if he decided to go back into approved education or training (such as college) and reduced his working hours – you may be able to make a new claim at that point.

I hope this has been helpful in clarifying your situation. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old, which means he falls into the 16-19 age category where Child Benefit can only continue if the child remains in approved education or training. However, Alex has finished school and is not currently in any approved education or training. Additionally, Alex is working full-time (more than 24 hours per week) at a local shop. Under the Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While the claimant meets the basic residency requirement for Child Benefit, Alex does not meet the criteria for continued eligibility because he is over 16, is no longer in approved education or training, and is working full-time (24+ hours per week). Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex under these circumstances.

Under the Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While the claimant meets the basic residency requirement for Child Benefit, Alex does not meet the criteria for continued eligibility because he is over 16, is no longer in approved education or training, and is working full-time (24+ hours per week). Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex under these circumstances.
